mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values indicate accelerating inflation, which forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening economic momentum and reduced corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of months where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with positive outcomes occurring in > 50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production growth exceeding 2% year-over-year, expanding manufacturing output supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 2% fails to generate positive 5-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the predictive power vanishes outside the early-cycle regime.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and consumer spending capacity, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions do not correlate with positive 20-day returns, with negative returns dominating in > 60% of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 4.5%, borrowing costs for corporations and consumers rise sharply, compressing profit margins and equity multiples, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising GDP and falling unemployment indicates a strong economic expansion that boosts corporate earnings forecasts, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising GDPC1 and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with negative outcomes exceeding 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, declining industrial production signals supply-side constraints and stagflation risks, which severely penalizes equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative returns do not dominate when INDPRO falls during high CPIAUCSL periods, or the mechanism shows no predictive edge in the specified regime.
